channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New Orleans Parish assistant DA dies by apparent suicide in office: report
item.description: Ian Kersting, a 34-year-old Parish prosecutor near New Orleans, was found dead with "apparent self-inflicted gunshot wounds" at his office, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KREX 5

item.title: New Orleans mourns victims of truck attack with tearful vigil and celebration of life
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans mourned, wept and danced at a vigil Saturday evening along the famous thoroughfare where a man rammed a pickup truck into a crowd, killing and injuring revelers who were there to celebrate the new year.
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, JACK BROOK, STEPHEN SMITH and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers brace for Trump's promised Jan. 6 pardons. Some are urging restraint
item.description: The fourth anniversary of the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol has a new focus as lawmakers brace for the prospect that Donald Trump may soon pardon many of the more than 1,500 people charged with crimes for their actions related to the riot.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago Bulls plan to retire Derrick Rose's jersey next season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Derrick Rose wasn't sure how he will feel when sees his No. 1 hanging from the rafters. He was still trying to process the news. The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday they plan to retire Rose's jersey sometime next season.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, ABC 4

item.title: Filmmaker and husband to Aubrey Plaza, Jeff Baena, dies by suicide: report
item.description: Film writer and director Jeff Baena, who is also known for his marriage to Hollywood actress Aubrey Plaza, has died at the age of 47, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: New Orleans attacker’s apparent loyalty to Islamic State group highlights persistent threat of lone wolf terrorism
item.description: The deadly Jan. 1, 2025, attack in New Orleans serves as a reminder of the persistent threat to the U.S. from individuals inspired by extremist Islamist groups.
item.dc:creator: Sara Harmouch, American University, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans mourns victims of truck attack with tearful vigil and celebration of life
item.description: New Orleans has held a vigil on Bourbon Street to mourn and celebrate the lives of those killed when a man rammed a pickup truck into a crowd, killing and injuring revelers celebrating the new year.
item.dc:creator: Jim Mustian, Jack Brook, Stephen Smith And Sara Cline, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IRS reminds taxpayers in 24 disaster area states of 2025 deadlines
item.description: A tax filing extension for residents of two dozen states affected by disasters is coming to an end in 2025, the IRS reminded taxpayers Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Selena's killer, Yolanda Saldívar, files for parole, begins review process
item.description: Saldívar, now 64 years-old, was sentenced to life in prison on Oct. 26, 1995, with the possibility of parole after 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Mendez, WGN 9

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rudy Giuliani insists he's not hiding assets at contempt hearing over $148 million judgment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani seemed to favorably impress a judge with three hours of testimony Friday at a contempt hearing as he insisted he's not hiding assets from lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment for two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In-N-Out opening new restaurants across several states in 2025 and beyond: Here's where
item.description: The burger chain, which does not franchise its restaurants, currently keeps its operations (mostly) to the Western half of the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jill Biden got most expensive gift from a foreign leader in 2023. Here's what it was
item.description: President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamond from India’s le
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former employee charged with child abuse after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s NICU
item.description: A former employee of Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) has been charged with malicious wounding and child abuse after an investigation into babies being found with "unexplainable fractures" at the NICU.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: Thomas has agreed to follow updated requirements on reporting trips and gifts, including clearer guidelines on hospitality from friends.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Biden says he will block US Steel sale to Nippon Steel
item.description: President Biden announced Friday he would block the sale of U.S. Steel to Japanese-owned Nippon Steel following a yearlong review of the potential acquisition. “We need major U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: A data company has figured out which airlines fly on time most often
item.description: Aeromexico is the most on-time airline in the world, according to a data company that compiled figures for 2024. Cirium said in its annual ranking released on Thursday that nearly 87% of the Mexican airline's flights were on time in 2024.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden awards the 2nd highest civilian award to leaders of the Jan. 6 committee and 18 others
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday awarded the second highest civilian medal to Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of choking reporter in racially-motivated attack
item.description: "The allegations in this case are very concerning," District Court Deputy DA Victoria Fazzino said. "The people have victim-safety concerns (and) community safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI says driver in New Orleans rampage acted alone and was '100%' inspired by Islamic State group
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The Army veteran who drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers in New Orleans acted alone, the FBI said Thursday, reversing its position from a day earlier that he likely worked with others in the deadly attack that officials said was inspired by the Islamic State g
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck explosion marks second high-profile incident at Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: The Cybertruck fire and explosion was preceded by a 2021 bomb threat that was meant to serve as a message to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, Fox 5

item.title: Survivor of New Orleans terror attack recalls 'surreal' aftermath of deadly attack that shattered his legs
item.description: Jeremi Sensky was returning to his hotel after meeting friends in the early hours on New Year’s Day when he heard a "massive noise" — the last thing he remembers before ending up facedown on the ground with his wheelchair smashed around him.
item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas and Doha Madani, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man pushed onto NYC subway tracks recovering while suspect is charged with attempted murder
item.description: A man survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an incoming train in New York City on New Year's Eve and is expected to fully recover, relatives said, while the person accused of pushing him was being held without bail Thursday on attempted murder and assault charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, Fox 5

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Death toll in New Orleans attack rises to 15, coroner says; more details emerge as investigation continues
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 15 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, church leader asks for prayers for victims of truck attack
item.description: At a Mass celebrated at New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, a church leader acknowledged the sense of unease that remains after the New Year's Day truck attack in the French Quarter and asked for prayers for the victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2
